events.chronicle: A water main older than the borough let go under #street# in {year}. {neighborhood} got #main_gift#.
The steam line under #street# went up on a #weekday# in {year}. #steam_consequence#
Engineers condemned the #bridge_name# Bridge in {year}. Traffic disagreed for #duration#, then the bridge settled the argument.
A sinkhole opened on #street# in {year}, #sinkhole_size#. #sinkhole_consequence#
The freeze of {year} held for #duration#. #freeze_consequence#
The blizzard of {year} dropped #snow_depth#. The plows #plow_excuse#.

residue.grammar: a list of names taped inside a bus shelter, laminated against the weather of {year}

events.chronicle: A fever walked out of a {neighborhood} cooling tower in {year}. #outbreak_consequence#
events.news: TOWER FLU: DON'T BREATHE THE MIST

events.chronicle: The rats reorganized in {year}. {neighborhood} exterminators described the colonies as 'coordinated' and declined follow-up questions.

residue.grammar: a hard hat filled with cement and plastic roses, set where the cornice came down in {year}

events.chronicle: A cornice let go over #street# in {year}. #collapse_consequence#
Smoke from #smoke_source# sat on the city for #duration# in {year}. The sun went small and orange and everyone photographed it like it was new.
Rolling brownouts owned the summer of {year}. The grid kept office hours. #brownout_consequence#
The outfall reversed in {year} and the harbor returned a decade of deposits to {neighborhood}. Listings called it 'waterfront character.'
Number #building_number# #street# stopped existing on a #weekday# morning in {year}. The gas company's lawyers beat the fire trucks by eleven minutes.
The {neighborhood} landfill caught in {year} and went on burning underground for #duration#. The hills steam in winter. Locals don't name it.
A microburst took {neighborhood} apart for ninety seconds in {year}. #microburst_loss# went up. Most of it came down.
The salt front reached the intakes in {year} and the tap water turned estuary. The bottled-water men became briefly, intensely important.
events.news: CITY ON THE ROCKS: TAP GOES BRACKISH

events.chronicle: The river found the {neighborhood} tunnel in {year}. The pumps fought for #duration#, then filed for retirement.
The scaffolding over #street# entered its #scaffold_year# year in {year}. A generation has been born, schooled, and married under it. The permit is current.
The {year} tremor lasted eleven seconds. Damage was minor. Theology was extensive.
The harbor went green off {neighborhood} for #duration# in {year}. The scientists said algae. The attendance said otherwise.

main_gift: a geyser, briefly the borough's tallest landmark
free rapids and a boil order
a lake with a parking problem
water_graffiti: THE PIPES ARE OLDER THAN THE LIES
steam_consequence: The crater steamed for a month and acquired a fan club.
Keep your windows shut, said the flyers, three days late.
Three manhole covers were never recovered. One cleared a four-story building.
sinkhole_size: the size of a brownstone footprint
sinkhole_consequence: The city fenced it. The fence became a tollbooth within the week.
Things were thrown in. Lists were thrown in.
It swallowed a parked cruiser and made the front page as a hero.
Engineers found older brick down there, and under that, older brick.
freeze_consequence: Pipes burst in alphabetical order, or so it felt.
The warming centers filled, then the precincts, then the churches, in that order of last resort.
Heating oil moved under armed escort, like it had earned it.
tabloid_freeze: CITY ON ICE: WHO GETS THE HEAT?
snow_depth: enough to lose a car in
plow_excuse: had been sold for scrap the previous spring, it emerged
made it as far as the depot exit
outbreak_consequence: Forty blocks learned to cough into their elbows and distrust mist.
The tower was drained, blessed, and drained again.
Masks came back like an old fashion.
The health department's map had a hole where the data should be.
collapse_consequence: The sidewalk shed went up the next day and will outlive us all.
Inspection records surfaced, pre-signed, in bulk.
The bricks were sold as souvenirs within the month.
smoke_source: pine barrens burning two states south
the refinery fires across the water
an upstate landfill fire nobody claimed
brownout_consequence: Ice became a currency with a melt rate.
Elevator buildings emptied downward, floor by floor.
The wattmen raised rates at dusk, like tides.
Sleep moved to the roofs, and the city got quieter and meaner at once.
microburst_loss: Two roofs, a steeple, and every umbrella in the neighborhood
The pier shed and a flock of laundry
A revival tent and three billboards
Most of a scrapyard, sorted by weight
